title: S&P 500 Closes Above 7,000 for First Time Ever; Nasdaq 12-Day Win Streak
detail: The S&P 500 closed at 7,022.95 on April 15, 2026, crossing the 7,000 level for the first time in history. The Nasdaq simultaneously notched its 12th consecutive positive session — the longest winning streak since 2009. Markets surged despite the active US naval blockade of Iran, driven by AI/tech stocks (which account for roughly half of S&P 500 market capitalization) and optimism that a second round of US-Iran peace talks would produce a deal before the ceasefire expired on April 21. The rally represented a remarkable reversal from the approximately 9-10% selloff that followed the April 6 Section 232 tariff escalation, with the S&P recovering to record highs within just 10 days.

media.caption: S&P 500 closes at historic 7,022.95 on April 15, 2026 — crossing 7,000 for the first time ever

title: ICE Releases FY2025 Data — 442,637 Removals; Sets 1 Million Annual Deportation Target
detail: ICE released its official FY2025 deportation statistics on April 15, revealing 442,637 migrants formally removed in fiscal year 2025 (October 2024 through September 2025), with only 166,939 having criminal records beyond illegal entry. For FY2026 mid-year data (through April 4), ICE reported 234,236 formal removals. More significantly, the agency's congressional budget justification quietly included a target of 1 million deportations per year for FY2026-FY2027, with a 500,000/year criminal deportation goal and 400,000/year arrests target. A separate Washington Post analysis found that as of April 4, 70.8% of ICE detainees — 42,722 of 60,311 — had no prior criminal conviction, contradicting the administration's stated focus on criminal deportations.

media.caption: ICE releases FY2025 deportation data and sets ambitious 1 million annual removal target for FY2026-FY2027

title: Trump: Iran War 'Very Close to Over'; Second Talks Round Expected 'in Days'
detail: President Trump told reporters on April 15 that the Iran war was 'very close to over' and predicted the US stock market 'is going to boom' after a deal is reached. In a CBS News phone interview, Trump said Iran had 'agreed to everything' on uranium enrichment, while a White House official separately described prospects for a new deal as 'good' — though Trump had not yet formally extended the ceasefire beyond its April 21 expiry. The Pentagon confirmed the Hormuz naval blockade was 'fully implemented.' Trump said a second round of talks could happen 'in days,' maintaining pressure on Tehran while leaving a diplomatic off-ramp open.

media.caption: Trump says Iran war 'very close to over' and predicts market boom — April 15, 2026
